sections.label: Intro
sections.description: The opening; bring up energy gradually, no full kick pattern or lead yet.

sections.label: Breakdown
sections.description: Energy dip mid-track; drums often drop out so a melodic motif can sit alone.

sections.label: Drop
sections.description: The energy peak; full pattern arrives, kick and bass commit, the groove locks in.

sections.label: Mid Break
sections.description: A low-energy middle section between peaks; drums often drop out.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: The closing tail; strip elements down so the track mixes cleanly into the next.

templates.label: Club Tool
templates.description: Long evolving structure with two energy peaks.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Sparse percussion only; built for DJs to mix into from the previous track.

sections.label: First Build
sections.description: A short rising section that tightens tension into the first peak.

sections.label: First Peak
sections.description: High-energy zone where elements stack near their fullest.

sections.label: Mid Break
sections.description: Low-energy interlude; drums often drop out so a textural moment can breathe.

sections.label: Second Peak
sections.description: The fullest version, usually denser than the first; all elements at maximum.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Wind down; strip back to drums and one textural element for the DJ mix-out.

templates.label: Standard
templates.description: Intro → first drop → mid-section break → second drop.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Atmospheric or melodic opening; no full breakbeat yet, often just kick/bass.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: The breakbeat arrives; bass voice commits; usually stripped relative to the second drop.

sections.label: Mid Break
sections.description: Energy dip mid-track; drums step down or drop out, melodic content carries the section.

sections.label: Second Drop
sections.description: The full payoff; bass at its widest, drums at their hardest.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Drums and bass step back; melodic tail or pad lingers to mix out.

templates.label: Free-form
templates.description: No rigid template. Three phases of evolving texture.

sections.label: Opening
sections.description: First exposed material; establishes the sonic world without rigid pulse.

sections.label: Development
sections.description: Slow transformation of the opening material; introduce new layers gradually.

sections.label: Dissolution
sections.description: The piece quietly disassembles itself; elements drop until only texture or silence remains.

templates.label: Free-form
templates.description: No template. Four phases of statement and transformation.

sections.label: Opening
sections.description: First exposed material; establish the sonic vocabulary.

sections.label: Statement
sections.description: A concentrated assertion of the central idea, usually mid-piece.

sections.label: Development
sections.description: Transform, fragment, or recombine the statement; introduce contrast.

sections.label: Dissolution
sections.description: The piece disassembles into texture, silence, or unrecognizable material.

templates.label: Free-form
templates.description: Through-composed, deliberately resistant to rigid section labels.

sections.label: Opening
sections.description: Establish the rhythmic and timbral palette; let the listener orient.

sections.label: Statement
sections.description: Concentrated articulation of the central rhythmic or melodic idea.

sections.label: Development
sections.description: Process the statement: glitch, displace, reframe, or invert.

sections.label: Dissolution
sections.description: Final unraveling; the material disintegrates into texture or silence.

templates.label: Standard
templates.description: Intro, two drops with a mid-track break.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Atmospheric setup; kick and sub bass establish the pulse, no full bass voice yet.

sections.label: Build
sections.description: Tension rises into the first drop; riser FX, snare rolls, filter sweeps.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: First commitment to the full bass voice; usually stripped for impact contrast.

sections.label: Mid Break
sections.description: Low-energy middle section; drums step down or drop entirely.

sections.label: Second Drop
sections.description: The fullest version; layered bass, denser drum programming.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Tail; strip back to pad or atmospheric loop.

templates.label: Standard
templates.description: Intro, build, half-time drop, second drop.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Atmospheric or rhythmic setup; usually no bass voice yet.

sections.label: Build
sections.description: Rising tension; snare rolls, risers, pre-drop signaling.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: Drums hit half-time; bass voice commits. Often more restrained than the second drop.

sections.label: Mid Break
sections.description: Energy resets; bass drops out, melodic material or vocal chops can shine.

sections.label: Second Drop
sections.description: Full bass design at its widest; usually the heaviest moment in the track.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Drums and bass thin out; texture or pad carries the tail.

templates.label: Uplifting
templates.description: Long build, anthemic breakdown, double drop.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Sparse rhythmic opening for DJ mixing.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: Sets up the chord progression at moderate density; lead is usually a single voice.

sections.label: Breakdown
sections.description: Drums drop, the anthemic melodic theme is exposed in full.

sections.label: Build
sections.description: Tension climbs back; supersaw stacks layer in, snare rolls accelerate.

sections.label: Main Drop
sections.description: Full supersaw stack over the same progression as the first drop; the emotional peak.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Strip back to drums and a single pad for DJ-friendly mix-out.

templates.label: Single Pattern
templates.description: One 16-step 303 line arranged purely by filter automation.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Drums and the 303 enter with filter mostly closed; sparse and dark.

sections.label: Filter Opens
sections.description: Slow filter automation upward; the squelch becomes audible.

sections.label: Peak
sections.description: Filter near fully open, resonance pushed; the 303 is loudest and most acidic.

sections.label: Filter Closes
sections.description: Reverse the build; filter automation downward, energy bleeds away.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Drums step out; 303 fades or holds a single note.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Skipping drums establish the swing; bass enters in the second 8 bars.

sections.label: Verse (2-step)
sections.description: 2-step kick pattern; syncopated, off-kilter feel.

sections.label: Chorus (4/4)
sections.description: Snap to four-on-the-floor; the hook arrives, energy locks straight.

sections.label: Break
sections.description: Drums thin; melodic chops or vocal cuts carry the moment.

sections.label: Final Chorus
sections.description: Return to 4/4; usually adds a counter-melody or stacked vocal.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Strip to drums for the DJ to mix out.

templates.label: Continuous Build
templates.description: Rolling bass throughout, melodic layers add and dissolve.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Atmospheric pads and a slowly rising kick; rolling bass not yet committed.

sections.label: Bass Roll
sections.description: The relentless 16th-note off-beat bassline locks in; the rhythmic engine of the track.

sections.label: Melodic Layer
sections.description: A lead motif arrives above the bass-roll without changing the rhythmic engine.

sections.label: Peak
sections.description: All layers stack; lead, percussion, FX at their busiest.

sections.label: Dissolve
sections.description: Layers thin one by one; bass-roll fades, atmosphere takes over.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Texture-only tail; kick still pulsing for DJ mix-out.

templates.label: Standard
templates.description: Intro, build, two drops separated by a switch.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Atmospheric or melodic opening; 808 enters but hi-hats not yet committed.

sections.label: Build
sections.description: Hi-hat rolls accelerate; risers and snare fills signal the drop.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: Full 808 pattern lands; hi-hats rolling at trap speed; sparse melodic content.

sections.label: Switch
sections.description: Short transition where the drum pattern shifts and the bass voice changes character.

sections.label: Second Drop
sections.description: Variation on the first drop; new bass character or additional melodic layer.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Drums fade; final 808 tail or atmospheric loop closes.

templates.label: Standard
templates.description: Vocal-chop chord stabs across a verse-chorus shape.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Sparse vocal chops over a soft pad; no drums yet or just a kick pulse.

sections.label: Verse
sections.description: First lyrical or melodic statement; drums enter at half-time.

sections.label: Build
sections.description: Risers, vocal pitch-bend, snare rolls into the drop.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: Full chord-stab synth voice over four-on-the-floor or half-time drums.

sections.label: Mid Break
sections.description: Vocal-chop only, drums step back; transitional space.

sections.label: Second Drop
sections.description: Variation: new synth voice or additional layer over the same chord progression.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Pad and final vocal chop fade out.

templates.label: Pop Form
templates.description: Verse / chorus / bridge, borrowed from 80s pop.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Arpeggiated pad establishes the chord progression; no drums yet.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.description: First melodic statement; lead enters, drums at moderate density.

sections.label: Chorus 1
sections.description: Hook section; the main melodic and harmonic statement.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.description: Second statement; usually adds a layered texture (gated snare, supporting lead).

sections.label: Chorus 2
sections.description: Repeat hook; conventionally the gated reverb snare arrives here for impact.

sections.label: Bridge
sections.description: A contrasting departure; new chord progression or sparser texture before the final chorus.

sections.label: Final Chorus
sections.description: Final hook; lead often lifted an octave or doubled for the climactic statement.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Pad and arp tail out; reverb wash carries the final seconds.

templates.label: Standard
templates.description: Build, kick-roll, drop with reverse-bass, second drop.

sections.label: Intro
sections.description: Pad and anthem lead establish the chord progression; no kick yet.

sections.label: Build
sections.description: Rising tension; kick enters, snare rolls accelerate.

sections.label: Kick Roll
sections.description: Descending pattern of pitched kicks that rolls into the drop, signalling impact.

sections.label: First Drop
sections.description: Full kick pattern at hardstyle BPM; anthem lead at full stack.

sections.label: Break
sections.description: Drums drop out; pad and lead breathe; reverse-bass not yet introduced.

sections.label: Second Drop (reverse-bass)
sections.description: Reverse-bass enters as the defining voice; the peak of the track.

sections.label: Outro
sections.description: Kick and lead thin out; pad tail carries the close.
